_scope: General — cross-project notes and memory system documentation only. Project-specific drafts live in projects.<name>.* chunks.

entries.title: Comparative Analysis — Memory Solutions for Claude
entries.summary: A survey of memory architectures available for or applied to Claude, their design philosophies, tradeoffs, and how the MemoryAccess GitHub-based system fits within the landscape. Includes optimization candidates derived from open-source solutions.

solutions.name: Claude Native Memory (claude.ai)
solutions.mechanism: Automatically scans chat history and generates a synthesized summary every 24 hours. Also supports explicit 'remember this' commands. Implemented as two tool calls: conversation_search and recent_chats.
solutions.scope: Personal/user-level. Projects get separate memory spaces.

strengths: Available to all tiers as of March 2026

solutions.name: Claude Code — CLAUDE.md + Auto Memory
solutions.mechanism: CLAUDE.md: user-written instruction files per project, stored in version control. Auto Memory: Claude writes notes to ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/.

solutions.name: Anthropic Memory Tool (API)
solutions.type: Client-side file-based memory via tool calls
solutions.mechanism: Claude makes tool calls (create/read/update/delete) on a /memories directory. Just-in-time context retrieval primitive.

solutions.name: MCP-based External Memory (e.g. Pieces, XTrace)
solutions.type: External structured memory via MCP connectors
solutions.mechanism: Memory stored in external systems, exposed to Claude via MCP tool calls. Real-time capture.

solutions.name: claude-mem (Claude Code plugin)
solutions.mechanism: Captures Claude coding sessions, compresses with AI, injects relevant context into future sessions.

solutions.name: MemoryAccess — GitHub-based Structured Knowledge Base (this system)
solutions.mechanism: Knowledge stored as JSON chunks in a GitHub repo. Session loader fetched at session start. Index files consulted before any chunk load. Chunks fetched on demand.
solutions.scope: General memory system. User-owned. Platform-agnostic in principle. Open-ended: project findings may eventually inform the memory architecture itself.
solutions.design_principle: Deliberate access over ambient loading. User and Claude co-author the knowledge base.

entries.positioning_notes: MemoryAccess sits closest to Claude Code's CLAUDE.md philosophy but extends it with explicit domain structure, on-demand loading, and a session loader protocol. Main gap relative to MCP-based solutions is absence of semantic retrieval.

optimization_candidates.name: Tiered metadata on index entries
optimization_candidates.description: Add importance, last_updated, topic tags to index entries for smarter lookup decisions.

optimization_candidates.name: Explicit online vs offline operation separation
optimization_candidates.description: Make fast/slow operation distinction an explicit design principle guiding future decisions.

optimization_candidates.name: Context eviction policy
optimization_candidates.description: Rule for when a loaded chunk is considered done and no longer worth carrying in active context.

optimization_candidates.name: Stale chunk deprecation
optimization_candidates.description: Flag rarely-accessed entries as stale in index; archive rather than delete.
